Music: Wagner's Ride of the Valkyries I drank 1-3 cups of this coffee every morning, and the occasional evening, for about 4 years while living in the seattle area where Ladro is located. All of their coffee is premium, and there is a lot of good coffee produced in the area. I moved back to california in 2019 and brought about ten pounds back with me because I could not find a way to order it at the time, it is possible I just didn't know where to look. Anyway, This coffee is some of the best shit you can acquire without breaking customs laws and ending up a side character in a vin diesel movie. Highly recommend you try it if you haven't before and are not turned off by dark roasts.

trying to dial in a new Breville machine. I went through a couple bags of Starbucks I got from the store and the only way I could drink it was to drown it in milk. My first double shot of Tony's Espresso Noir was tasty. I'm happy with that.

5 lbs bag of this to try to revive my espresso journey. I am incapable of giving a highly descriptive review of this coffee as I am still in the process of trying to figure out my pallete. That is, I’m no longer just throwing some milk on top of my shots and calling it good. What I can say is this, for espresso this was seems more acidic than I would have otherwise thought. I am not saying this in a bad way, as I was actually bracing myself for a very bitter brew. The roast is clearly dark and oils can be seen on the beans, evidencing the second crack. All in all, I was pleased with this roast and think that it was an all-in-all good roast to restart my espresso journey with. I mainly buy lift roasts for drip coffee, but that stuff was straight battery acid in my espresso machine. I think this was a good all around roast for espresso.
